title: Intensely Umami Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A light and comforting Algerian dish featuring sweet sherry and fermented ingredients, perfect for a sick day, with an ayurvedic-rajasic friendly twist.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb pieces with cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Brown the lamb pieces on all sides, then remove them from the pot.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the sweet sherry, miso paste, and prunes to the pot, stirring to combine.
Return the browned lamb pieces to the pot and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the tagine for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
